message:
fix(model): preserve original casing in Status/IssueType FromStr Custom fallback

Fine-tune the FromStr impls on Status and IssueType so unknown values
fall through to `Custom(original_string)` instead of
`Custom(lowercased_string)`. Keeps the existing case-insensitive
matching for known variants while guaranteeing round-trip stability
for user-supplied values outside the canonical set.

Before:
  "QaReview".parse::<Status>() -> Status::Custom("qareview")
After:
  "QaReview".parse::<Status>() -> Status::Custom("QaReview")

Implementation: the `to_lowercase()` call is hoisted into a
`normalized` local used only for the match arms; the `_` fallback
now clones the original `s`. Same pattern applied to IssueType.

Why it matters: downstream tooling that reads an Issue's status back
to render it (TUI badges, robot JSON output, tree views) used to see
a silently lower-cased string for every non-canonical value,
breaking display parity with the JSONL on disk and confusing users
who hand-edited their statuses.

Adds a regression test in the existing test module:
`Status::from_str("QaReview")` must equal `Status::Custom("QaReview")`
— would have returned `Status::Custom("qareview")` before this fix.
